market_context: Markets are under extreme pressure as President Trump's 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaches. Futures are down 0.4-0.6% with oil prices spiking above $110/barrel. Geopolitical uncertainty is driving risk-off sentiment across all asset classes.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are down 0.5% premarket as geopolitical tensions escalate ahead of Trump's Iran deadline. The index has been in a falling trend with technical resistance at $596, and volume divergence signals suggest further downside despite recent gains.

QQQ premarket at 586.59 shows weakness below yesterday's close of 588.50. Technical analysis shows mixed signals with RSI at 64.47, but the 200-day MA at 595.10 suggests sell pressure. Nasdaq futures down 0.6% indicate tech weakness.

Apple shares already plunged 4% on reports of foldable iPhone delays per Nikkei. Engineering setbacks could delay mass production by months, weighing on investor sentiment. CEO Cook's recent $16.5M stock sale adds to negative sentiment.

Oil prices surging above $110/barrel as Trump's Iran deadline approaches and US strikes Kharg Island oil export hub. WTI trading at $113-114 range suggests continued energy sector strength. Geopolitical premium likely to persist through deadline.

VIX already surged 13% to 27.7 as geopolitical tensions mount. With Trump's Iran deadline just hours away and escalating threats of infrastructure strikes, fear gauge likely to spike further as uncertainty peaks before 8 PM ET deadline.
